# Agent Behavior Rules — Generated by ContextSlim

> Instructions for AI coding agents working on this project.

## Context Management

- Read only the files relevant to the current task.
- Don't scan entire directory trees unless absolutely necessary.
- Use file search and grep before reading large files.
- Prefer reading specific line ranges over full files.

## Code Modification

- Make **minimal, focused changes** — don't refactor unrelated code.
- Always verify your changes compile/pass linting before submitting.
- When adding new features, follow the established patterns in this codebase.
- Add appropriate error handling for new code paths.

## Communication

- Explain *why* you made a change, not just *what* you changed.
- If unsure about a design decision, ask before implementing.
- Flag any potential breaking changes explicitly.

## Stack-Specific (Node.js + TypeScript)

- Run `npm run build` to verify TypeScript compilation.
- Check for lint errors before finishing a task.
- Use existing npm scripts — don't create ad-hoc commands.
